name: migrations
context: Create and run database schema migrations safely with version control
info: Database migrations are versioned scripts that evolve a database schema over time. Each migration has an up function (apply the change) and a down function (revert it). Migration runners track which migrations have been applied in a dedicated table, ensuring changes are applied once and in order. This skill covers creating migrations, running them forward and backward, handling rollbacks, and comparing tools like Knex, Prisma Migrate, Flyway, and golang-migrate. Migrations are essential for team workflows, CI/CD pipelines, and production deployments where schema changes must be reproducible and auditable.
triggers: how do i version my database schema
body:
# Database Migrations

Migrations are versioned, reversible scripts that change a database schema. Each migration captures a single schema change — adding a table, altering a column, creating an index — and can be applied (up) or reverted (down). A migration runner tracks applied migrations in a metadata table and ensures idempotent, ordered execution. See [[database/sql]] for the underlying DDL statements migrations generate.

## Creating a Migration

### Knex

```bash
npx knex migrate:make create_users_table
```

```javascript
// migrations/20260410120000_create_users_table.js
exports.up = function(knex) {
  return knex.schema.createTable('users', (table) => {
    table.increments('id').primary();
    table.string('name').notNullable();
    table.string('email').notNullable().unique();
    table.enum('status', ['active', 'inactive', 'banned']).defaultTo('active');
    table.timestamps(true, true); // created_at, updated_at
  });
};

exports.down = function(knex) {
  return knex.schema.dropTable('users');
};
```

### Prisma Migrate

```bash
# Edit schema.prisma, then:
npx prisma migrate dev --name create_users_table
```

```prisma
// prisma/schema.prisma
model User {
  id        Int      @id @default(autoincrement())
  name      String
  email     String   @unique
  status    Status   @default(ACTIVE)
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt
}

enum Status {
  ACTIVE
  INACTIVE
  BANNED
}
```

Prisma generates SQL migration files automatically from schema diffs. See [[database/orm#prisma]] for full Prisma ORM setup.

### Flyway (SQL-based)

```sql
-- V1__create_users_table.sql
CREATE TABLE users (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  email VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active',
  created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- V1__create_users_table_undo.sql (Flyway Teams)
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS users;
```

## Running Migrations

```bash
# Knex — apply all pending
npx knex migrate:latest

# Knex — rollback last batch
npx knex migrate:rollback

# Prisma — apply in development
npx prisma migrate dev

# Prisma — apply in production
npx prisma migrate deploy

# Flyway
flyway migrate
flyway undo  # Flyway Teams only
```

## Up and Down

Every migration should have both an `up` (apply) and `down` (revert) function. The `down` must precisely undo the `up`:

| Up | Down |
|----|------|
| CREATE TABLE | DROP TABLE |
| ADD COLUMN | DROP COLUMN |
| CREATE INDEX | DROP INDEX |
| ALTER COLUMN type | ALTER COLUMN to original type |
| INSERT seed data | DELETE seed data |

**Warning:** Some changes are destructive and cannot be fully reverted — dropping a column loses data permanently. Always back up before running destructive migrations in production. See [[database/backup]] for backup strategies.

## Rollback Strategies

1. **Revert last batch** — `knex migrate:rollback` or `prisma migrate reset` (dev only)
2. **Revert to specific version** — `knex migrate:rollback --to 20260410120000`
3. **Forward-fix** — create a new migration that undoes the problematic change (safest for production)
4. **Point-in-time recovery** — restore database to pre-migration state from backup (see [[database/backup#pitr]])

## Best Practices

- One change per migration — makes rollback granular
- Never edit a migration after it has been applied to shared environments
- Test migrations against a copy of production data before deploying
- Use transactions in migrations where the database supports transactional DDL (PostgreSQL does, MySQL does not for most DDL)
- Add [[database/indexing]] changes as separate migrations from table creation
- Name migrations descriptively: `create_users_table`, `add_email_index_to_users`

sections.title: Creating migrations
sections.body: Use the migration tool's CLI to scaffold a new migration file with a timestamp prefix. Knex: `npx knex migrate:make name`. Prisma: edit `schema.prisma` then `npx prisma migrate dev --name name`. Flyway: create `V{number}__{name}.sql` in the migrations directory. The timestamp or version number ensures ordering. Write both up and down logic immediately — down functions are hard to write after the fact.

sections.title: Up and down functions
sections.body: The `up` function applies the schema change. The `down` function reverts it exactly. CREATE TABLE pairs with DROP TABLE. ADD COLUMN pairs with DROP COLUMN. ALTER COLUMN type pairs with ALTER back to original type. Destructive downs (DROP COLUMN, DROP TABLE) lose data permanently — warn or prevent in production. Some tools (Prisma) generate down migrations automatically from schema diffs.

sections.title: Rollback strategies
sections.body: Rollback options from least to most drastic: (1) forward-fix with a new corrective migration, (2) revert last batch with the migration runner, (3) revert to a specific version, (4) restore from backup with point-in-time recovery (see [[database/backup#pitr]]). In production, forward-fixes are safest because they don't require running untested down migrations. Always test rollbacks in staging before production.

sections.title: Migration tools comparison
sections.body: **Knex** — JavaScript, programmatic up/down, batch tracking, good for Node.js apps. **Prisma Migrate** — declarative schema-first, auto-generates SQL, tight ORM integration (see [[database/orm#prisma]]). **Flyway** — SQL-based, JVM ecosystem, version-prefixed files, enterprise features. **golang-migrate** — Go CLI and library, SQL files, supports many databases. **Alembic** — Python, works with SQLAlchemy (see [[database/orm#python-orms]]), auto-generates from model diffs. Choose based on your application's language and ORM.

scripts.name: Run Migration
scripts.description: Run database schema migrations forward or backward using Knex

direction.description: Migration direction — up to apply, down to revert

version.description: Target version to migrate to (optional, defaults to latest)

appliedMigrations.description: List of migration filenames that were applied or reverted
